mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods following a rate decline, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns (accuracy < 50%), or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during periods of stable inflation signals robust economic capacity utilization, generating positive equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% annually, or predictive accuracy drops below 45% in high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monthly inflation spikes trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in >55% of cases following CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4%, or signal loses predictive power during disinflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, supporting corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of observations, or interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero in panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue fundamentals, consistently forecasting positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth rates correspond to negative 20-day forward returns in >55% of cases, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal immediate supply-side contraction and weakening economic momentum, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances following two consecutive INDPRO declines, or signal accuracy drops below 40% in out-of-sample periods.
